title: Functions, Callbacks, Context, and Memory Interfaces
description: A plugin registry invokes callbacks after their contexts change ownership and one implementation uses an incompatible signature. Learners cumulatively produce typed callback and context registry through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
Bind callback, context pointer, ownership, thread, and unregister lifetime into one interface contract.
Inject matched allocate, resize, and release operations with context and failure semantics.
